data.description:
## Incident Overview

**Scenario Type**: Test authoring footgun
**Source**: /dso:end-session technical-learnings sweep, 2026-04-28 sprint session (epic 6f7a-4ba6)

### Expected Behavior
When writing tests that call handle_status_event() via Python subprocess, passing a directory path for reducer_path should fail visibly or work correctly.

### Actual Behavior
Passing Path(repo_root) / 'plugins' / 'dso' / 'scripts' (a directory) to reducer_path causes importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to return None, which causes reduce_ticket() to return None, which causes compiled_status to be None, which silently skips the delete branch. The test passes (no assertion error) but delete_issue is never called.

### Root Cause
bridge-outbound.py uses Path(__file__).resolve().parent / 'ticket-reducer.py' — a file path. Test fixtures must match: Path(repo_root) / 'plugins' / 'dso' / 'scripts' / 'ticket-reducer.py'.

### Fix
Always pass the full .py file path for reducer_path in tests and production callers.

## Component
plugins/dso/scripts/bridge/_outbound_handlers.py, tests/acceptance/test-ticket-delete-e2e.sh
data.title: [bridge test fixtures]: reducer_path passed as directory -> importlib.util.spec_from_file_location returns None -> test silently skips delete path
